case.direct_skill_invocation: $ops-observability-card Show token, cost, latency, supplied metrics, and service-quality gaps for this loop.
case.hermes_chat_prompt: Use OMH ops-observability-card for: Show token, cost, latency, supplied metrics, and service-quality gaps for this loop.
case.user_value: Operators can see health/cost/service-quality signals without confusing supplied exports with live provider truth or completion evidence.
case.evidence_boundary: An ops observability card is not billing truth, provider quota truth, live metric-provider access, complete tracing, SLO pass, incident closure, remediation completion, performance proof, or workflow completion evidence.
case.preferred_usage: Use as an installed Hermes workflow skill when operators need an evidence-bounded command-board for telemetry, supplied metric-provider payloads, and service-quality gaps.

boundary: Use-case demo cards are wrapper rendering artifacts. They prove OMH can shape a Hermes-facing card for the scenario; they are not runtime, connector, delivery, file, memory, or execution evidence.
